summary.label: Autonomous care layer
summary.detail: Designed for future robots that can patrol, refill, dose nutrients and capture proof.
summary.detailZh: 面向未来机器人巡检、补水、加营养液和采集凭证。

summary.label: 24h watch
summary.detail: Robots do not replace expert technicians first; they detect issues and handle repeatable routines.
summary.detailZh: 机器人先做异常发现和重复动作，不一开始替代专家技师。

summary.label: Hardware moat
summary.detail: Wall modules need robot-readable IDs, refill ports, dosing ports, leak sensing and safe access lanes.
summary.detailZh: 绿墙模块需要机器人可读 ID、补水口、加液口、漏水检测和安全通道。

summary.label: Proof upgrade
summary.detail: Every robotic patrol can create photos, timestamps, module readings and operation logs.
summary.detailZh: 每次机器人巡航都能生成照片、时间戳、模块读数和操作日志。

capabilities.label: Autonomous patrol
capabilities.body: A service robot follows approved indoor routes and checks wall appearance, leak risk, light status and access blockers.
capabilities.opsLink: Creates sensor, proof and incident records in the OPS ledger.

capabilities.label: Photo proof capture
capabilities.body: The robot captures repeatable full-wall and zone photos for AI health scoring and client reporting.
capabilities.opsLink: Feeds Health Score, Proof Vault, ESG and renewal packs.

capabilities.label: Water refill
capabilities.body: The robot connects to standardized refill ports and logs flow volume, refill duration and leak checks.
capabilities.opsLink: Updates water ledger, health drivers and exception audit trail.

capabilities.label: Nutrient dosing
capabilities.body: Nutrient cartridges let the robot dose by recipe while preserving batch traceability and safety limits.
capabilities.opsLink: Connects supply inventory, dosing log and plant recovery trend.

capabilities.label: Human escalation
capabilities.body: The robot escalates pruning, replacement, pest confirmation and customer-sensitive issues to human technicians.
capabilities.opsLink: Creates work orders with photos, likely cause and recommended next action.

moduleInterfaces.label: Robot-readable module ID
moduleInterfaces.requirement: QR, NFC or visual marker on each wall/module lets the robot bind actions to the correct asset.
moduleInterfaces.reason: Without a stable ID, refill, dosing and proof records cannot be trusted.

moduleInterfaces.label: Refill and dosing port
moduleInterfaces.requirement: Standardized physical ports with mechanical alignment and permission control.
moduleInterfaces.reason: Autonomous water and nutrient actions need a safe, repeatable docking interface.

moduleInterfaces.label: Leak and overflow guard
moduleInterfaces.requirement: Flow meter, leak sensor, backflow protection and auto-stop threshold.
moduleInterfaces.reason: Hong Kong FM clients will care deeply about water risk in offices, clinics and show suites.

moduleInterfaces.label: Plant-only camera zone
moduleInterfaces.requirement: Camera framing, cropping and privacy controls keep people out of routine proof capture.
moduleInterfaces.reason: Privacy and client approval are prerequisites for robotic photo proof.

moduleInterfaces.label: Indoor robot route map
moduleInterfaces.requirement: FM-approved routes, elevator rules, docking point, charging point and no-go zones.
moduleInterfaces.reason: A robot can only become a care layer if it fits building operations.

fleetScenarios.body: Good first pilot because the reception route is controlled, the wall is visible and the client values report proof.
Strong health-safety value, but patient privacy and clinic access rules require strict approval.
High brand value during sales campaigns; robot proof can protect presentation quality before VIP visits.
Useful later, but member experience and moving obstacles make it a less obvious first pilot.

workflow.body: Robot follows FM-approved route, scans asset ID and confirms it is facing the correct wall module.
Robot captures plant-only photos, checks leak/light/tank status and uploads evidence to Proof Vault.
If permitted, robot connects to the port, applies volume or nutrient recipe and records the operation.
OPS compares photos, sensor state and service rules to decide whether to keep watching or create a work order.
Technicians handle trimming, replacement, pest confirmation, client-sensitive actions and physical exceptions.

roadmap.label: Robot-ready data model
roadmap.body: Asset ledger, health score, proof vault, work orders and ESG claim boundaries are already structured for future automation.
roadmap.investorSignal: The current MVP creates the operating data that robots will later use.

roadmap.label: Standard wall interfaces
roadmap.body: Define module ID, refill port, dosing cartridge, leak guard, camera framing and privacy rules.
roadmap.investorSignal: This turns DR FOREST Wall into hardware that a robot can service repeatably.

roadmap.label: Robot pilot
roadmap.body: Pilot night patrol and photo proof in one controlled client site before enabling refill and dosing.
roadmap.investorSignal: A pilot proves 24h care without claiming full autonomy too early.

roadmap.label: Autonomous living asset network
roadmap.body: Scale robots across routes, modules and clients with human exception teams and evidence-led reporting.
roadmap.investorSignal: The long-term story becomes recurring FM revenue plus robotics-enabled operating leverage.

evidencePack.label: Robot patrol log
evidencePack.body: Stores route, time, asset ID, battery state, photos and detected exceptions.
evidencePack.proof: Route log + photo proof + asset scan

evidencePack.label: Water refill record
evidencePack.body: Records water volume, refill duration, target module and leak-check result.
evidencePack.proof: Flow meter + module ID + leak sensor

evidencePack.label: Nutrient dosing record
evidencePack.body: Links recipe, cartridge batch, dose amount and plant recovery trend.
evidencePack.proof: Cartridge ID + dose log + health score trend

evidencePack.label: Robot photo proof
evidencePack.body: Repeatable photos improve health score consistency and client report credibility.

evidencePack.label: Human exception handoff
evidencePack.body: Shows where robot monitoring ends and human technician responsibility begins.
evidencePack.proof: Work order + technician approval + closure photo

claimControls.label: Allowed: robotics-ready architecture
claimControls.labelZh: 可主张：机器人就绪架构
claimControls.body: Use this now: DR FOREST OPS is designed to support future robotic patrol, proof capture, refill and nutrient dosing.

claimControls.label: Allowed after pilot: robotic patrol proof
claimControls.labelZh: 试点后可主张：机器人巡检凭证
claimControls.body: Use robot patrol, photo and exception logs only after a controlled site pilot proves reliable operation.

claimControls.label: Blocked: full autonomy today
claimControls.body: Do not claim fully autonomous plant care, 24h guaranteed remediation, or human replacement before hardware pilots and safety validation.

claimControls.label: Required: human and safety fallback
claimControls.body: Every robot action needs permission rules, leak safety, privacy controls and a human escalation path.
